idea.hook: Why does ANYTHING exist in our Universe?
facts: The Big Bang should have created perfectly equal amounts of matter and antimatter.
If equal, every particle would have annihilated with its antiparticle, leaving only pure energy.
Scientists estimate for every BILLION antimatter particles, there was 1,000,000,001 matter particles.
idea.payoff: This minuscule, unexplained imbalance—just one extra matter particle per billion antimatter particles—is why galaxies, stars, planets, and YOU exist today!
idea.title: Universe's EXISTENCE Mystery! ✨🧐

scenes.visual: Abstract cosmic background with swirling nebulae and faint stars. Question marks subtly appear and dissolve.
A visual representation of the Big Bang expansion, quickly followed by a perfectly balanced scale. One side labeled 'MATTER' (blue), the other 'ANTIMATTER' (red), both level.

scenes.text: Equal amounts = Total Annihilation. Pure Energy.
scenes.visual: Blue and red particles (representing matter and antimatter) rapidly collide and disappear in flashes of light, leaving an empty, pure energy void (bright white flash fading to dark).
A swarm of tiny red particles (antimatter) fills most of the screen. Then, one distinct blue particle (matter) appears and pulses, slightly larger than the rest, highlighting the ratio.

scenes.text: This tiny imbalance is why YOU exist!
scenes.visual: Zoom out from the single blue particle, rapidly expanding to show a galaxy, then stars, then a planet (Earth), and finally a silhouette of a person looking up at the stars. The background becomes a vibrant galaxy.
